questions.q: What is the difference between primary, secondary, and tertiary amines?
questions.answer: Primary amines have one alkyl group attached to the nitrogen atom, secondary amines have two alkyl groups, and tertiary amines have three alkyl groups.

questions.q: How do amines react with nitrous acid?
questions.answer: Amines react with nitrous acid to form diazonium salt and water, which further decomposes to form nitrogen gas, water, and an aromatic compound.

questions.q: What is the functional group of amines?
questions.answer: The functional group of amines is the amino group (-NH2).

questions.q: What is the difference between aromatic and aliphatic amines?
questions.answer: Aromatic amines are attached to a benzene ring, while aliphatic amines are not attached to a benzene ring.

questions.q: How do amines react with sodium?
questions.answer: Amines react with sodium to form sodium amide and hydrogen gas.

questions.q: What is the effect of substituents on the basic strength of amines?
questions.answer: Substituents on the basic strength of amines are as follows: -CH3 is electron-donating, and -NO2 is electron-withdrawing. Electron-donating substituents increase the basic strength, while electron-withdrawing substituents decrease the basic strength.

questions.q: Write the reaction of aniline with bromine water.
questions.answer: Aniline reacts with bromine water to form 2,4,6-tribromophenylamine.

questions.q: Describe the preparation of aniline from nitrobenzene.
questions.answer: Aniline is prepared from nitrobenzene through reduction with iron and hydrochloric acid to form phenylhydrazine, which is then reduced with sodium amalgam to form aniline.

questions.q: Explain the importance of amines in medicine.
questions.answer: Amines are used as local anesthetics, analgesics, and antihistamines. They are also used as drugs for the treatment of various diseases such as Parkinson's disease, schizophrenia, and depression.

questions.q: Describe the preparation of alkyl halides from amines.
questions.answer: Alkyl halides are prepared from amines by reaction with sodium nitrite and hydrochloric acid to form diazonium salt, which is then treated with copper(I) chloride and a halogen to form alkyl halide.

questions.q: Explain the effect of temperature on the basic strength of amines.
questions.answer: The basic strength of amines decreases with an increase in temperature. This is because the amino group becomes less basic as the temperature increases.

questions.q: Describe the reaction of amines with alkyl halides.
questions.answer: Amines react with alkyl halides to form N-alkylated amines. This reaction is known as alkylation.

questions.q: Explain the importance of amines in the production of plastics.
questions.answer: Amines are used as monomers in the production of plastics such as polyurethane, polyamide, and polyurea.

questions.q: Describe the preparation of amides from amines.
questions.answer: Amides are prepared from amines by reaction with acyl chlorides or anhydrides in the presence of a base.

questions.q: Explain the effect of substituents on the basic strength of amines.
questions.answer: Substituents on the basic strength of amines are as follows: -CH3 is electron-donating, and -NO2 is electron-withdrawing. Electron-donating substituents increase the basic strength, while electron-withdrawing substituents decrease the basic strength.

questions.q: Describe the reaction of amines with acids.
questions.answer: Amines react with acids to form salts and water. This reaction is known as neutralization.
